daily_brief: The US-Iran conflict dominated global headlines as American forces completed their seventh consecutive night of strikes targeting Iranian military infrastructure, surveillance sites, and maritime capabilities [BBC, France 24, Globe and Mail]. Tehran responded by launching missiles at US allies including Kuwait, where two water and electricity plants were damaged, and Jordan, which intercepted ten Iranian missiles [France 24, The Hindu]. Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz has largely halted, threatening roughly one-fifth of global oil and LNG supplies [BBC]. In Ukraine, President Zelensky's surprise dismissal of popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov sparked protests across the country and outrage among soldiers, with troops telling the BBC they feel betrayed by the decision amid ongoing conflict with Russia [BBC]. Meanwhile, Canadian wildfires continued to send smoke across the northern United States, prompting President Trump to threaten new tariffs on Canada over what he called 'willful negligence' in forest management [BBC, Globe and Mail, Al Jazeera]. The UK political landscape shifted as Labour confirmed Andy Burnham as party leader and incoming Prime Minister, while in Russia, authorities detained prominent anti-war blogger Ilya Remeslo and moved to bar politician Boris Nadezhdin from parliamentary elections [RFI, BBC].

top_stories.headline: US Completes Seventh Night of Strikes on Iran as Regional Conflict Expands
bullets: US Central Command struck Iranian surveillance sites, military logistics infrastructure, underground weapons storage, and maritime capabilities overnight
Iran retaliated by launching missiles at US allies including Kuwait (damaging two water/electricity plants), Jordan (which intercepted 10 missiles), and Bahrain
Shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has largely stopped; the waterway normally handles about one-fifth of global oil and LNG supplies
Iran claimed two oil tankers exploded after hitting mines in the strait, which US Central Command denied as false

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focused on military operations and shipping disruption

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized US media reports of military preparing further escalation

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Detailed the tit-for-tat nature of strikes and infrastructure targeting

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Content unavailable due to technical issues

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Iranian claims of striking US targets in Kuwait and Jordan

top_stories.headline: Zelensky's Dismissal of Popular Defence Minister Sparks Protests and Military Outrage in Ukraine
bullets: President Zelensky declined to reappoint Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov in his cabinet reshuffle, nominating Maj-Gen Yevhenii Khmara as acting replacement

Fedorov revealed he had suggested Zelensky replace Commander-in-Chief Syrskyi and Chief of General Staff Hnatov; Zelensky acknowledged 'systemic' conflict between the General Staff and defence ministry
Protests erupted in Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities with demonstrators holding signs reading 'Hands off Fedorov' and 'Stop sabotaging victory'
Soldiers interviewed by BBC expressed outrage, with one calling it 'a blatant slap in the face to all service members'

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focused on soldier reactions and quotes describing sense of betrayal

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Same coverage, emphasized political tensions between military leadership

top_stories.headline: Trump Threatens New Tariffs on Canada Over Wildfire Smoke Crossing US Border
bullets: President Trump accused Canada of 'willful negligence' in forest management as smoke from approximately 888 Canadian wildfires blanketed northern US states
Trump announced plans to call Prime Minister Mark Carney demanding an explanation and threatened to add costs to existing tariffs
Over 190 fires are burning in Ontario alone, with the majority out of control
Republican lawmakers renewed calls to make Canada the 51st US state; Ontario Premier Doug Ford had asked the US for firefighting support rather than complaints

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Balanced coverage noting both Trump's threats and Canadian Premier's request for assistance

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Supreme Court constraints on Trump's tariff powers and detailed Michigan Republicans' prior complaints

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Focused on air quality impact on World Cup final preparations

top_stories.headline: Russia Intensifies Crackdown on Anti-War Dissent, Detaining Blogger and Barring Politician
bullets: Blogger Ilya Remeslo, a former Putin supporter who called the president a 'war criminal and thief' in March, was remanded in custody for two months on suspicion of spreading false military information
Politician Boris Nadezhdin was convicted of 'displaying extremist symbols,' barring him from collecting signatures for September parliamentary elections
Nadezhdin had previously attempted to run for president on an anti-war platform in 2024 but was blocked over signature irregularities
Most genuine opposition figures have fled Russia or, like Alexei Navalny, died in custody

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Provided context on broader pattern of opposition suppression

top_stories.headline: Andy Burnham Confirmed as UK Labour Leader, Set to Become Prime Minister Monday
bullets: The 56-year-old 'King of the North' faced no challengers for Labour leadership after securing backing from 379 of 403 Labour MPs
Burnham returns as an MP after a nine-year absence, having served as Greater Manchester mayor since 2017
He replaces Keir Starmer and becomes the UK's seventh prime minister in a decade
Burnham will meet King Charles III on Monday to formally receive the invitation to form the next government

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Emphasized his soft-left positioning and hopes he can communicate better than Starmer

additional_stories.headline: Ukrainian drone attack kills seven at Moscow logistics centre, wounds 24, as Russia claims 370 drones launched overnight
Venezuela earthquake death toll exceeds 5,000 as IMF releases $346 million in emergency reconstruction funds
White House defends Argentina football team's right to wave Falklands sovereignty banner at World Cup, citing First Amendment
EU proposes relaxing emissions trading rules, allowing some industries until 2038 instead of 2034 to reduce carbon output
Gang violence in Haiti expands beyond Port-au-Prince, killing at least 61 people including 14 children in Kenscoff attacks
German politician Jens Spahn faces resignation calls after revealing he became a parent via US surrogacy despite party's ban on practice
Seven American aid workers quarantining at controversial Kenya Ebola facility after US travel restrictions from Congo
France and England meet in World Cup third-place match as Didier Deschamps ends 14-year tenure as Les Bleus coach
IEA warns critical minerals investment fell 9% in 2025, with supply chains increasingly concentrated in China and Indonesia
Flash floods kill four and leave four missing in Vietnam's Lai Chau province after days of heavy rain
